chain.thought:
Add necessary columns to the table.
The question asks about how many clerks started before a certain year. We need to know the starting year of each clerk. We extract the value from column "started" and create a different column "start year" for each row. The datatype is datetype.

Select relevant rows.
The question aims to find out the number of clerks who started their clerkship before the year 1959. We need to look at the relevant rows in the table where the "started" column has a value less than 1959. The rows that meet this condition are row 1, row 2, row 3, and row 4.

Filter out useless columns.
similar words of the question link to columns:
started -> started
column value of the question link to columns:
1959 -> started, finished
semantic sentence of the question link to columns:
clerks started before 1959 -> started

The question is asking to determine the number of clerks who started their service before the year 1959. Since each row represents an individual clerk and the "started" column indicates the year they began their service, we can evaluate the "started" column to see which clerks have a start year less than 1959. We can use the f_group_column(started) operation to group the clerks based on their start year to analyze this situation.

The question is asking to determine how many clerks started before the year 1959. To find out, we need to sort the data based on the "started" column in the order of "small to large" using the f_sort_column(started) operation. This will allow us to see which clerks started before 1959 by checking the sorted list of starting years.

The clerks who started before 1959 are Kenneth W. Dam (1957), Alan C. Kohn (1957), William C. Canby, Jr. (1958), and Heywood H. Davis (1958). There are no clerks who started before 1957. Therefore, the total number of clerks who started before 1959 is 4.
